title: ProMock LIVE Mock 10 - Sectional Timer P

questions.question: Find the odd one out from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: Except ‘violin’ all are precussion instruments.

questions.question: If ‘BRAIN’ is coded as ‘YI13M’, how is ‘CAMEL’ Coded?
questions.explanation: ⇒ Consonants have opposite letter in alphabetical series, whereas vowels one coded as number of their respective position as a vowel ⇒ Therefore, CAMEL is coded X1N2O.

questions.question: Find the missing term in the series. B, C, E, G, …..
questions.explanation: Alphabets are positioned as consecutive prime numbers. B (2), C(3), E(5), G(7), …… Therefore, next number will be K(11).

questions.question: You are given a Master Address (MA) form official records. Your task is the identify the correct candidate address that matches exactly with the master address in terms of formatting, punctuation, spelling, and structure. Master Address (MA) : Flat no. 5B-12/3, Green Park Residency, Near city mall, Pune – 411001. Candidate Address:
options: Flat No. 5B-12/3, Green Park Residency, Near City Mall, Pune – 411001
Flat No. 5B-12/4, Green Park Residency, Near City Mall Pune – 411001
Flat No. 5B-12/3, Green Park Res., Near City Mall, Pune – 411001
Flat No. 5B-12/3, Green Park Residency, Near City Mall, Pune, 410011
questions.explanation: Check options: - • Option 2 has wrong Flat No. (Flat No. 5B-12/4 instead of 5B-12/3) • Option 3 has Res. Instead of Residency. • Option 4 has wrong Pincode (410011 instead of 41001) ⇒ Option 1 matches the exact address.

questions.question: Directions: these questions are based on the following information: ‘A @ B’ means ‘B is the mother of A’ ‘A $ B’ means ‘B is the husband of A’ ‘A # B’ means ‘B is the sister of A’ ‘A & B’ means ‘B is the son of A’ If ‘P # Q $ R & S # T’ then how is Q related with T?
questions.explanation: → Therefore, Q is the mother of T

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the fifth number in the same way as the second number is related to the first number and the fourth number is related to the third number. 7 : 15 :: 9 : 21 :: 3 : ?
Which of the following numbers would replace the question mark (?) in the given number series? 10, 30, ?, 130, 222.
questions.explanation: Solution : Observe the pattern : n³+n Check : 2³+2=10 3³+3=30 5³+5=130 6³+6=222 ⇒ So, the missing term = 4³+4=68

questions.question: A number has been denoted to each of the given letters. Select the option from the following four possible arrangements of these numbers that forms a meaningful word. M=6, O=3, L=1, N=4, E=5
questions.explanation: 15634=L E M O N

questions.question: Select the option that represents the correct order of the given words as they would appear in an English dictionary. 1. Blanket 2. Blossom 3. Blade 4. Blame 5. Blink 6. Block
questions.explanation: Correct order: 1. Blade 2. Blame 3. Blanket 4. Blink 5. Block 6. Blossom

questions.question: Direction: Choose the correct option form the alternative 64 : 8 ∷ 52 : ?

Each item describes a situation and is followed by Four possible responses. Indicate the response you find most appropriate. An employee has been feeling extreme stress of work. The manager Suggests taking short breaks and managing workload Gradually, but the employee wants to finish everything quickly in one go. What is the most appropriate choice?
options: Ignore stress and continue working nonstop.
Follow the managers advice and manage workload gradually
Rely on caffeine and push through the stress
questions.explanation: Following a gradual workload plan is the safest and most sustainable way to manage stress, as it presents burnout and supports long-term productivity.

questions.question: If CHROME is coded as HPROEZ, how is ORANGE coded?
questions.explanation: Therefore,

questions.question: Office assistant (Temporary) Eligibility Criteria (as on 01-01-2026): 1. Minimum 21 years old. 2. Must have complete graduation in any discipline. 3. Should have basic computer knowledge of (MS office) 4. Must be willing to work for at least 6 months. Case: Rohit is 22 years old and has completed his graduation in commerce. However, he does not have basic computer knowledge, and he mentions that he can only work for 4 months due to personal commitments. Question: What decision should be made in Rohit’s case?
options: Refer the case to the HR Manager
Select, provided the learn computer Skills quickly.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: 2 Logic: Candidate fails mandatory criteria Computer knowledge + minimum duration. So, reject.

questions.question: Chose the word that is least like the other words in the group.
questions.explanation: Influenza, Dengue and measles are viral disease. Whereas, Tuberculosis is a bacterial disease.

questions.question: Complete the analogy. Teacher : Knowledge ∷ Doctor : ?
questions.explanation: Correct Answer : A Logic : A teacher imparts Knowledge, similarly a doctor deals with medicine (care domain / essence of their role).

questions.question: Instruction: Chose the inferences that are clearly Supported by the statement. Statement: All employees to be selected for the senior manager position must have at least 10 years of work experience, and Strong leadership skills are also required. Inferences: Inferences I → An employee with 10 years of work experience and strong leadership skills will be selected as a senior manager. Inference II → Having 10 year of work experience alone is not sufficient for selection as a senior manager.
options: Both Inference I and II follows
Neither Inference I nor II follows.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer : Option 2 Logic : The statement gives necessary conditions, not guarantees. So, I does not follows., but II correctly states that only experience is not sufficient other factors are also there.

questions.question: Arrange as per dictionary order and select the word that is second in order: BRACE, BRAND, BRAKE, BRAVE
questions.explanation: Arrange alphabetically: - BRACE → BRAKE → BRAND → BRAVE. So, second is BRAKE. Correct answer: option 1) BRAKE.

questions.question: 8 Women P, Q, R, S, T, U, V and W are sitting around a circular table Facing opposite to the centre (not necessarily in the same order). Q is second to the left of W. P is third to the left of Q. S is the immediate right of W. U is fourth to the left of S. R is the immediate neighbor of W and Q. V is second right of W. Who is the immediate right of T?
questions.explanation: ⇒ Therefore, U is to the immediate right of T.

questions.question: In the following questions, in each alternative a group of numbers is given. The numbers of each group are related to the numbers of the same group by Same logic/ Rule/ Relation. Select the group of numbers in which numbers are not related by that same logic/ Rule/ Relation. (Note : Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking blow the number into its constituent digits. E.g 13 – operations on 13 such as adding/ subtracting / multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations an 1 and 3 is not allowed)
questions.explanation: Patterns ⇒ Second – First = (Third)³ Option 1) 1142-142=10³ 1000=1000 ✔ Option 2) 2364-167=13³ 2197=2197 ✔ Option 3) 2957-213=14³ 2744=2744 ✔ Option 4) 3525-175=15³ 3550≠3375 X ⇒ Therefore, Option 4 is different from others

questions.question: By inter changing the given tow signs which of the following equation will NOT be correct? × and ÷ I. 72÷6×3+8-4=40 II. 90×5÷3+6-1=59
questions.explanation: Interchange X and ÷ I. 72×6÷3+8-4=40 144+8-4=40 148≠40 X II. 90÷5×3+6-1=59 18×3+6-1=59 59=59 ✔ Final answer : Option 1) Only I is not correct.

questions.question: In the question below there are three statements followed by two conclusions I and II. You have to take the three given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance from commonly known facts and them decide which of the given conclusions logically follows form the three statements disregarding commonly known facts. Statements : Some Night are dark. All dark are ancient. No dark is vedic. Conclusions : I. Some Night is Vedic II. Some Night is not Vedic.
options: Both Conclusion I and II follows
Neither conclusion I nor II follows
questions.explanation: Correct answer: Option 4) only conclusion II follows

questions.question: If 119 “ 80 = 13 and 196 “ 13 = 61, then what is the value of 119 “ 17 = ?
Which Article of the Constitution of India deals with the oath or affirmation of the Vice President of India?
questions.explanation: Articles Related to Vice President of India • Article 63 There shall be a Vice President of India. • Article 64 The Vice President shall be ex-officio Chairman of the Council of States (RajyaSabha). • Article 65 The Vice President acts as President when the office of the President is vacant or when the President is unable to perform duties. • Article 66 Election of the Vice President. • Article 67 Term of office of Vice President (5 years) and resignation/removal. • Article 68 Time of holding election to fill vacancy in the office of Vice President. • Article 69 Oath or affirmation by the Vice President. • Article 70 Discharge of President’s functions in other contingencies. • Article 71 Matters relating to election disputes of President and Vice President are decided by the Supreme Court.

questions.question: The Warli painting tradition gained international recognition, largely due to the efforts of which of the following renowned painters?
questions.explanation: Warli painting is a tribal art form from Maharashtra. It was traditionally done by Warli women for rituals and weddings. Jivya Soma Masheis credited with popularizing Warli painting globally by converting the ritual art into a modern art form and exhibiting it internationally. Important Paintings / Art Styles and Their Pioneers • Warli Painting – Jivya Soma Mashe Tribal painting from Maharashtra, white figures on mud background. • Madhubani Painting – Sita Devi Folk painting from Bihar, includes mythological themes and bright colors. • Bengal School of Art – Abanindranath Tagore Developed modern Indian painting style, opposed Western art style. • Modern Indian Art – Amrita Sher-Gil Known as pioneer of modern Indian art. • Miniature Painting Revival – Nandalal Bose Important figure in Indian traditional and miniature art revival. • Mughal Painting – Abd al-Samad One of the early Mughal court painters under Akbar. • Rajput Painting – Nihal Chand Famous for BaniThani painting.

questions.question: Which chemical reaction produces ortho-hydroxybenzoic acid, the primary reaction product used as a rawmaterial for medications?
questions.explanation: Kolbe’s reaction (Kolbe–Schmitt reaction) is used to prepare salicylic acid (ortho-hydroxybenzoic acid) from sodium phenoxide and carbon dioxide under pressure. Salicylic acid is an important raw material for medicines such as aspirin. Reimer–Tiemann reaction- This reaction is used to introduce a –CHO group (aldehyde group) into phenol. It mainly produces salicylaldehyde (o-hydroxybenzaldehyde). Phenol → Salicylaldehyde Stephen reaction- This reaction converts nitriles (R–CN) into aldehydes (R–CHO) using SnCl₂ and HCl. It is used for aldehyde preparation. Nitrile → Aldehyde Friedel–Crafts reaction- This reaction is used to introduce alkyl or acyl group into benzene ring using AlCl₃ catalyst. Two types: • Friedel–Crafts Alkylation • Friedel–Crafts Acylation Benzene → Alkylbenzene or Acylbenzene

questions.question: What is the name of India's indigenous long-range surface-to-air missile system under development,expected to be operational by 2028–29?
questions.explanation: Project Kusha is India’s indigenous long-range surface-to-air missile (LR-SAM) system being developed by DRDO. It is expected to be operational around 2028–29 and is intended to provide air defence against aircraft, missiles, and stealth fighters at long ranges. Agni-V – Intercontinental ballistic missile (nuclear-capable), not a surface-to-air missile. Trishul – Short-range surface-to-air missile project (now discontinued). Akash-NG – Next-generation Akash missile, but it is a medium-range SAM, not long-range like Project Kusha.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is/are true about Saraswati Samman? • The Saraswati Samman 2024 was awarded to Sanskrit scholar Bhadresh das Swami for his Sanskrit philosophical work Swaminarayan Siddhanta Sudha. • Mahasweta Devi was the first female recipient of the Saraswati Samman for her hindi literary work in 1996. • Amitabh Ghosh was the first English language recipient of the Saraswati Samman in 2018 for his book The shadow Lines.
questions.explanation: The SaraswatiSamman 2024 was awarded to Sanskrit scholar Bhadreshdas Swami for his Sanskrit philosophical work Swaminarayan SiddhantaSudha. Mahasweta Devi was the first female recipient of the SaraswatiSamman for her Bengali literary work in 1996. The first female winner in Hindi language for the SaraswatiSamman was Krishna Sobti for her work Zindginama. The first English SaraswatiSamman winner was Vikram Seth for A Suitable Boy. Amitav Ghosh received the first Jnanpith Award in English language in 2018 for his book The shadow lines.

questions.question: Which Chandela ruler is credited with the construction of the KandariyaMahadev Temple situated in Khajuraho, Madhya Pradesh, India?
questions.explanation: The KandariyaMahadev Temple is the largest and most ornate temple at Khajuraho and was built by the Chandela ruler Vidyadhara in the 11th century. The temple is dedicated to Lord Shiva and represents the peak of Chandela temple architecture. Extra Facts : Yashovarman → Built Lakshmana Temple, Khajuraho Dhanga → Built Vishwanath Temple, Khajuraho Vidyadhara → Built KandariyaMahadev Temple Chandela dynasty → Ruled Bundelkhand region (Madhya Pradesh)

questions.question: The Kyoto Protocol is related to the _____.
options: Control of transboundary movement of hazardous wastes.
questions.explanation: The Kyoto Protocol is an international treaty adopted in 1997 under the UNFCCC that aims to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and combat global warming. It legally binds developed countries to emission reduction targets. Important Environmental Protocols / Conventions • Kyoto Protocol – 1997 Purpose: Reduction of greenhouse gas emissions to control global warming. Related to climate change under UNFCCC. • Montreal Protocol – 1987 Purpose: Protection of the ozone layer by phasing out ozone-depleting substances like CFCs. • Paris Agreement – 2015 Purpose: Limit global temperature rise to well below 2°C above pre-industrial levels. • Cartagena Protocol on Biosafety – 2000 Purpose: Safe handling, transport and use of genetically modified organisms (GMOs). • Nagoya Protocol – 2010 Purpose: Fair sharing of benefits arising from genetic resources and biodiversity. • Basel Convention – 1989 Purpose: Control of transboundary movement of hazardous wastes. • Stockholm Convention – 2001 Purpose: Eliminate or restrict persistent organic pollutants (POPs). • Rotterdam Convention – 1998 Purpose: Regulation of international trade of hazardous chemicals and pesticides. • UN Convention to Combat Desertification – 1994 Purpose: Combat desertification and drought.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about the Industrial Policy Resolution 1948 in India: • The policy divided industries into four categories. • Railways were kept under complete state control. • The policy completely banned foreign capital investment.. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: The Industrial Policy Resolution 1948 was the first major industrial policy statement of independent India, announced on April 6, 1948. It laid the foundation for the country’s industrial development and mixed economy model. The 1948 policy classified industries into four groups: • Strategic industries (complete state monopoly) • Basic/key industries (state-controlled but private participation allowed) • Controlled private industries • Other industries (mainly private sector) Railways were included in strategic industries, which were fully owned and managed by the government. Other examples in this category included arms and atomic energy. The policy did NOT ban foreign investment. Instead, it allowed foreign capital, but with strict government regulation to protect national interests. The idea was to benefit from foreign expertise without losing control. Hence option 1 and 2 are correct.

questions.question: K-QUEST initiative which is primarily related to skill development and employment started by which state government?
questions.explanation: K-QUEST stands for a skill development initiative aimed at improving the employability of youth. It focuses on training in advanced technologies, innovation, and job readiness. The scheme is specifically implemented by the Government of Karnataka to boost employment opportunities in the state. These are flagship welfare schemes of the Karnataka government: • Shakti Scheme -Free bus travel for women in state-run buses. • Gruha Lakshmi Scheme -₹2000/month to women heads of BPL households. • Gruha Jyoti Scheme -Free electricity (up to a limit) for households. • Anna Bhagya Scheme -Free food grains (rice) for poor families. • Yuva Nidhi Scheme -Unemployment allowance for graduates/diploma holders. 2. Employment & Youth Schemes K-QUEST (Skill Development Initiative)-Training youth in advanced technologies and jobs. Yuva Spandana Program -Mental health and youth support services. Self Employment / Startup Schemes (Unnati Scheme) -Financial support for SC/ST entrepreneurs. . Women & Child Welfare Schemes Bhagyalakshmi Scheme -Financial aid for girl child welfare. Madilu Kit Scheme -Support kit for pregnant women. Thayi Bhagya Scheme -Free maternal healthcare for BPL women. 4. Health Schemes Arogya Karnataka (Vajpayee Arogyasri) -Free/affordable healthcare services. Cashless Snakebite Treatment Scheme (2025 update) -Free treatment for snakebite victims in hospitals. 5. Agriculture & Rural Development Ganga Kalyana Scheme -Irrigation support (borewells, water facilities). Krushi Aranya Protsaha Yojane (KAPY) -Incentives for tree plantation. Grama Vikasa / Rural Development Schemes - Village infrastructure and development.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a style of Carnatic music?
questions.explanation: Carnatic music (South Indian classical) includes Svarajati, Javali, Kriti, Varnam, Tillana, Alapana, etc. Hindustani music (North Indian classical) includes Chaturang, Khayal, Dhrupad, Thumri, Tarana. So, Chaturang does not belong to Carnatic music.

questions.question: Who won the gold medal in the men’s javelin throw at the 2025 World Athletics Championships held in Tokyo from 13–21 September 2025?
questions.explanation: Keshorn Walcott of Trinidad and Tobago won the gold medal with a throw of 88.16 m. Sachin Yadav (India) finished fourth with a personal best of 86.27 m but did not win a medal. Despite strong efforts and final appearances, India returned from Tokyo 2025 without a medal, marking a break in their medal streak at recent World Championships.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a key focus area of the 8th Five Year Plan (1992–1997) in India?
options: Complete nationalization of all private industries
questions.explanation: The 8th Five Year Plan emphasized: Growth with social justice Employment and poverty reduction Agriculture productivity and food security Industrial modernization and private sector participation Social sector development (education, health, infrastructure) It did NOT advocate the complete nationalization of private industries. In fact, post-1991 economic reforms encouraged private sector participation and liberalization, so option D is clearly incorrect.

questions.question: Match the following: Water harvesting techniques (Column A) Regional names / states (Column B) A) Cheruvus 1) Maharashtra B) Virdas 2) West Bengal C) Kulams 3) Andhra Pradesh D) Dighi 4) Tamil Nadu
questions.explanation: Correct matching is A – 3, B – 1, C – 4, D – 2 Cheruvus (Andhra Pradesh): Tanks for surface runoff and groundwater recharge. Virdas (Maharashtra): Step wells / tanks for water storage. Kulams / Ooranies (Tamil Nadu): Traditional tanks for irrigation and drinking water. Dighi / Pokhar (West Bengal): Ponds for irrigation and domestic purposes.

questions.question: According to the Global Gender Gap Report 2025 released by the World Economic Forum, what was the rank of India in the Global Gender Gap Index?
questions.explanation: In the 2025 report, India’s position was 131st out of 148 countries assessed. The index measures gender disparities across four dimensions: economic participation and opportunity, educational attainment, health and survival, and political empowerment.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a principal organ of the United Nations?
questions.explanation: The United Nations has six principal organs. These are: • United Nations General Assembly • United Nations Security Council • United Nations Economic and Social Council • International Court of Justice • United Nations Secretariat • United Nations Trusteeship Council The World Health Organization is not a principal organ. It is a specialized agency of the United Nations that works on global health issues.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the Apna Ghar Initiative launched in August 2025: • The scheme was launched by the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs • The main purpose of the scheme is to provide resting and basic facilities for truck drivers at highway fuel stations. • The scheme is mainly intended for migrant agricultural labourers. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Apna Ghar Initiative was launched by the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas to provide rest, food, toilet, and dormitory facilities for truck drivers at petrol pumps/highway fuel stations. The beneficiaries are truck drivers, not agricultural labourers.

questions.question: There is a circular coil having n turns. The field produced is how many times than produced by a coil of single turn?
questions.explanation: A shape formed by wrapping the electrical wires of copper or any other conductive material over a cylindrical tube of a dielectric material (eg. ceramic, glass or wood) is called a solenoid. When electric current in flowing on it, it starts behaving like a magnet. If it has more number of wire turns (n),then the magnetic field will also be higher that is we can say that the magnetic field generated by a coil with n round is compared to the magentic field generated by a single circled coil will be n times.

questions.question: Which book, launched by Union Minister Dharmendra Pradhan at the World Book Fair 2025, is part of the PMYUVA 2.0 scheme?
questions.explanation: At the World Book Fair 2025, Union Minister Dharmendra Pradhan launched several books under the PM YUVA 2.0 scheme. Among the given options, the book specifically released was “The Saga of Kudopali: The Unsung Story of 1857.”

questions.question: The collection of letters of Aurangzeb is known by which of the following names?
questions.explanation: Ruqaat-e-Alamgiri → Collection of Aurangzeb’s letters Ahkam-e-Alamgiri → Orders / regulations of Aurangzeb Dastur-ul-Amal → Administrative manual Zakhirat-ul-Khawanin → Biographical work

questions.question: Pandit Ratan Mohan Sharma is associated with which of the following gharanas?
questions.explanation: Pandit Ratan Mohan Sharma is associated with the Mewati Gharana. He is a Hindustani classical vocalist and a disciple and nephew of Pandit Jasraj, who belonged to the Mewati Gharana.

questions.question: Match the following: List I (Operations) List II (Country) A) Operation Rising Lion 1) India B) Operation True Promise III 2) Israel C) Operation Midnight Hammer 3) Iran D) Operation Sindhu 4) USA
questions.explanation: 1. Operation Rising Lion- Operation Rising Lion is associated with Israel. This operation refers to military action conducted by Israel, generally related to its security operations against militant groups or threats. The name is symbolic because the lion is a national symbol of Israel (Lion of Judah). 2. Operation True Promise III- Operation True Promise III is associated with Iran. “Iran” has used the name True Promise for its military operations, especially related to missile or drone operations in West Asia conflicts. 3. Operation Midnight Hammer - Operation Midnight Hammer is associated with United States. This refers to a US military operation, typically special forces / air strike type operation conducted at night (hence the name Midnight Hammer). 4. Operation Sindhu- Operation Sindhu is associated with India. This was an Indian operation, and such operations are usually related to evacuation missions, disaster relief, or military/logistics operations conducted by India.

questions.question: Assertion (A): A deficit in the current account of the Balance of Payments indicates that a country is importing more goods, services, and transfers than it is exporting. Reason (R): The current account of the Balance of Payments records only the export and import of physical goods.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true, but R is false
A is false, but R is true
questions.explanation: A is true, but R is false Current Account includes goods, services, income, and transfers (not only goods). If imports > exports → Current Account Deficit.

questions.question: Salkhan Fossil park is situated in which state?
questions.explanation: Salkhan Fossil Park is located in Sonbhadra district of Uttar Pradesh. It is one of the oldest fossil parks in the world, with fossils estimated to be around 1.4 billion years old, mainly stromatolite fossils (algae fossils).

questions.question: Which of the following statements is correct regarding Eukaryotic cell? I. Size generally small, around 0.1 to 1 μm II. There is a single chromosome in it
questions.explanation: Both Statements are incorrect. Eukaryotic cells are larger (10–100 μm). The size given (0.1–1 μm) is for prokaryotic cells. Eukaryotic cells have multiple chromosomes, not a single one.

questions.question: Select the correct statement about climax community.
options: It is an ecological community in which populations of plants or animals, which are very unstable and exist for very few time.
It is a very first community of ecosystem
It is an ecological community in which populations of plants or animals remain stable and exist in balance with each other and their environment.
It consist of only plant population that makes a new ecological system.
questions.explanation: Climax community = final stage of ecological succession It is stable, balanced, and self-sustaining Other options describe pioneer or unstable communities, not climax community.

questions.question: If the selling price of an article is increased by 20% and the profit percentage increases from 25% to 50%, what is the percentage increase in the cost price?
questions.explanation: CASE-1: Profit = 25%  CP : SP = 4 : 5 Let CP = 100 and SP = 125. CASE-2: New SP = 120% of 125 = 150 New Profit = 50% • New CP = 150 / 1.5 = 100 = old CP • Change in CP = 0%

questions.question: Two cards are drawn at random from a standard deck of 52 cards. What is the probability that both cards are red or both are face cards?
questions.explanation: P(E) = (no. of favourable outcomes)/(total no. of outcomes) Total no. of ways = 52C2 = 1326 No. of favourable outcomes: A + B – C A: both are red cards = 26C2 = 325 B: both are face cards = 12C2 = 66 C: common cards i.e., both are red face cards = 6C2 = 15 • No. of favourable outcomes = 376 P(E) = 376/1326 = 188/663

questions.question: A and B start a business with Rs. 30,000 and Rs. 50,000. After 4 months, A adds Rs. 10,000 more, and B withdraws one-fifth of his capital. If the total profit at the end of the year is Rs. 48,000, find A’s share.
If sin x = cos (2x + 30°), then find the value of x.
Simplify for n: (√5)6 ÷ (√5)8 = 5n.
Line L1 passes through (1, a) and (3, 5). Line L2 passes through (2, 2) and (6, 6). If L2 and L2 are parallel to each other, then find a.
questions.explanation: Slope = (y2 - y1)/(x2 - x1) Parallel lines have equal slopes. i.e., Slope1 = Slope2 (5-a)/(3-1) = (6-2)/(6-2) • 5-a = 2 • a = 3

questions.question: A right prism has a base that is a regular hexagon of side 7 cm. The height of the prism is 10 cm. A square cavity of side 4 cm is carved vertically through the center of the prism from top to bottom. Calculate the volume of the material removed from the prism.
questions.explanation: The removed material forms a square cuboid with a height equal to that of the main prism. Volume removed = Volume of cuboid = 4×4×10 = 160 cm3

questions.question: The average of 16 numbers is 25. If the sum of the first 8 numbers is 180, find the average of the remaining 8 numbers.
questions.explanation: Total sum = 16 × 25 = 400 Remaining sum = 400 − 180 = 220 Average = 220 / 8 = 27.5

questions.question: A sum amounts to Rs. 8000 in 4 years and Rs. 10000 in 6 years at simple interest. Find the rate of interest per annum.
questions.explanation: Difference in amounts = 10,000 - 8,000 = Rs. 2,000 • Interest per year = 2,000 / 2 = Rs. 1,000 Interest for 4 years = Rs. 4,000 • Principal = 8,000 - 4,000 = Rs. 4,000 SI = (P × R × T) / 100 so, Rate = (1,000 / 4,000) × 100 = 25%

questions.question: Find the value of: (a − b)(a + b) + (b − c)(b + c) + (c − a)(c + a) + ab + bc + ca − (a² + b² + c²).
options: a² + b² + c² − ab − bc + ca
questions.explanation: = (a² − b²) + (b² − c²) + (c² − a²) + ab + bc + ca − (a² + b² + c²) = 0 + ab + bc + ca − (a² + b² + c²) = − (a² + b² + c² − ab − bc − ca)

questions.question: Consider the following two statements, labeled Assertion (A) and Reason (R). Evaluate them and choose the appropriate answer from the given options. Assertion (A): Two circles that touch each other externally, have three common tangents Reason (R): One direct and two transverse tangents can be drawn between such circles.
options: Both A and R are true and R is not the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
A is false but R is true.
A is true but R is false.
questions.explanation: When two circles touch externally, they have 3 common tangents: • 2 Direct Common Tangents (the ones that pass "outside" both circles). • 1 Transverse Common Tangent (the one that passes through the point of contact between the two circles). So, A is true, but R is false.

questions.question: From the top of a tower 120 m high, the angles of depression of the top and bottom of a pole are 30° and 60°. Find the difference between the height of the pole and its distance from the tower (in m).
questions.explanation: AB: tower CD: pole of height ‘h’ x: distance b/w pole & tower Our required answer = |h – x| In △ABD, tan 60° = 120/x √3 = 120/x • x = 40√3 m In △AMC, tan 30° = AM/x 1/√3 = AM/40√3 • AM = 40 m • h = AB – AM = 80 m Answer = h – x = (80 – 40√3) m

questions.question: Two alloys contain copper and zinc in the ratios 4:1 and 5:2. If equal masses of both are melted and mixed, what is the ratio of zinc to copper in the final alloy?
questions.explanation: Let mass of each alloy be LCM (5, 7) = 35 Mass of: Copper = 4/5 of 35 + 5/7 of 35 = 53 Zinc = 1/5 of 35 + 2/7 of 35 = 17 • Ratio (Zinc : Copper) = 17:53

questions.question: Find the sub-duplicate ratio of 9:16.
questions.explanation: Sub-duplicate ratio of a : b = √a : √b Answer = √9 : √16 = 3 : 4

questions.question: A vendor mixes two rice types costing Rs. 20/kg and Rs. 40/kg in the ratio 3:2, but he mistakenly sells it at a loss of 10%. What is the selling price per kg (in Rs.)?
A rectangle of side 10 cm × 6 cm and a rhombus (acute angle 30°) are joined along one 6 cm side. What's the ratio of their areas (rectangle : rhombus)?
questions.explanation: Areas (rectangle : rhombus): • length × width : (side)2 × sin α • 10 × 6 : 62 ×sin (30°) • 10 : 6 × ½ • 10 : 3

questions.question: A man walks at a speed of √x km/h. After 3 hours, he has covered the same number of kilometers as the value of x. What is the distance travelled by the man after 6 hours?
questions.explanation: Using: distance = speed × time ATQ: x = 3×√x  x = 9 km/h So, distance travelled by the man in 6 hrs. = 6×√x = 18 km

questions.question: In ΔABC, the circumcenter is located at point O. The lengths of the sides are a, b, c. Which of the following statements about the distances from O is true?
options: Distance from O to side a is equal to distance from O to vertex A.
Distance from O to vertex C is equal to distance from O to vertex A.
Distance from O to side a is equal to distance from O to side b.
Distance from O to vertex B is half of distance from O to vertex C.
questions.explanation: O represents the circumcenter here, which is equidistant from all vertices. • OA = OB = OC Thus, distance from O to vertex C is equal to distance from O to vertex A.

questions.question: A store advertises, "Buy items worth Rs. 4000, get Rs. 500 off instantly." What is the effective discount percentage?
questions.explanation: Discount % = (discount amount)/(marked price)×100% = 500/4000×100% = 12.5%

questions.question: If a sum of Rs. 8000 is invested at 20% per annum compound interest, what will be the amount after 3 years?
questions.explanation: Amount = Principal × (1 + Rate%)time • Amount (after 3 years) = 8000 × 1.23 • Rs. 13,824

questions.question: A and B can complete a task in 15 days together. B is 20% more efficient than A. What percentage of the work is done by B alone?
questions.explanation: Efficiency ratio (A : B) = 5 : 6 Work done ∝ Efficiency here, Total work = 5 + 6 = 11 parts B’s share of work = 6 parts Percentage of the work is done by B alone = (6/11) × 100 = 54.54%

questions.question: Rational numbers are a subset of:
questions.explanation: Rational numbers are numbers of the form p/q (q ≠ 0). They include integers, fractions, terminating and recurring decimals. Thus, all rational numbers lie on the number line and are part of real numbers.

questions.question: If the radius of a sphere is increased by 3 cm, its surface area increases by 1056 cm2. What was the radius of the sphere after the increase?
questions.explanation: Surface area of sphere = 4πr2 ATQ: 4π(r+3)2 – 4πr2 = 1056 • 4 × (22/7) × (6r + 9) = 1056 • 2r + 3 = 28 • r = 12.5 Radius after the increase = 15.5 cm.

questions.question: Find the product of the modes of the following data set: 7, 2, 7, 2, 9, 4, 3, 3, 7, 5, 2, 6, 8, 2, 7, 5, 7, 2
questions.explanation: Mode = value(s) that occur most frequently in a data set. Here, frequency of: 2  5 times 7  5 times Product of the modes = 14

questions.question: If A, B and C are three numbers such that the HCF of A and B is A and the HCF of B and C is B, then the HCF of A, B and C is:
questions.explanation: HCF(A, B) = A • A divides B or B = m×A HCF(B, C) = B • B divides C or C = n×B If A divides B, and B divides C, then A must also divide C i.e., C = k×A Since, A is a factor of itself, a factor of B, and a factor of C, it is the greatest common factor for all three numbers. HCF(A, B, C)= A

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C). 👉 Replace "any other building" with "that of any building". 📌 Error concept: This is an error of Illogical Comparison. You cannot compare the "architecture" of a building directly to a "building" itself. You must compare architecture to architecture. Grammatical concept: When comparing a specific quality/part of one thing with that of another, we must use the pronoun "that of" (for singular nouns) or "those of" (for plural nouns) to maintain a logical comparison. Here, "architecture" is being compared to "building." To make it logical, we must compare the architecture of the Taj Mahal to the architecture (that of) of any building in London. • Note: We also remove "other" because the Taj Mahal is in India and London buildings are a different category; "any other" is used when comparing something within the same group. Corrected Sentence: The architecture of the Taj Mahal is more magnificent than that of any building in London.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The health department has been put on high alert following reports that a rare strain of the virus is likely to __________ in the densely populated suburban areas.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A). 📌 Explanation - The sentence refers to the sudden appearance and spread of a virus. The correct phrasal verb for the sudden start of something unpleasant, such as a disease, war, or fire, is "break out." Meanings and Explanation of Options: • Break out - (अचानक) फैलना / शुरू होना (बीमारी, युद्ध या आग) - To start suddenly (used for unpleasant things like diseases, wars, or fires). Example: Panic broke out when the fire alarm went off. • Break up - रिश्ता खत्म करना / टुकड़े-टुकड़े होना - To end a relationship; to break into small pieces; or for a meeting to end. Example: After three years of dating, they decided to break up. • Break into - जबरदस्ती घुसना (चोरी के इरादे से) - To enter a building by force, usually to steal something. Example: Burglars tried to break into the house while the family was away. • Break down - खराब होना (मशीन आदि) / रोने लगना - (Of a machine/vehicle) to stop working; or to lose emotional control and start crying. Example: Our car broke down on the highway.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. During the voting process, members are free to abstain from casting their ballots if they remain undecided.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D) - No improvement. 📌 Explanation - The sentence is grammatically correct. The verb "abstain" (meaning to choose not to do something) is always followed by the fixed preposition "from." Furthermore, a preposition is always followed by the Gerund form of the verb (V1+ing). Thus, "abstain from casting" is the correct structural usage. • Abstain from - से परहेज करना / से दूर रहना - To restrain oneself from doing or enjoying something. Example: He was advised to abstain from smoking.

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence -
options: One should be careful about what he says in a formal meeting like this.
The committee were divided in their opinion regarding the new policy change.
I have never seen such a man who is so dedicated to his work as him.
Not only the teacher but also the students was present at the annual function.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). 📌 Explanation - This sentence is grammatically correct. It follows the rule of Collective Nouns acting as individuals. When a collective noun like "Committee" is divided in opinion or acts separately, it is treated as a plural noun. Therefore, it correctly takes the plural verb "were" and the plural possessive adjective "their." Detailed Analysis of Other wrong Options: Option A: Error: Replace "he says" with "one says." The pronoun "One" is an indefinite pronoun that must be maintained throughout the sentence for consistency. You cannot switch to "he" or "him" if the sentence started with "One" as the subject. Option C: Error: Replace "as him" with "as he." In formal English, when a comparison is made using "as...as," the pronoun following the second "as" should be in the subjective (nominative) case because it is technically the subject of an implied verb. Option D: Error: Replace "was" with "were." This sentence follows the Rule of Proximity involving correlative conjunctions. When subjects are joined by "Not only... but also," the verb must agree with the subject closest to it.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks. The manager, along with his subordinates, has rarely displayed any signs of negligence during the execution of such high-stakes international projects, ________?
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C). 📌 Explanation - This question tests your knowledge of Question Tags combined with two specific rules: Subject-Verb Agreement with "along with" and Negative Adverbs. • Subject-Verb Agreement: When two subjects are joined by "along with," "as well as," or "together with," the verb (and the pronoun in the question tag) must agree with the first subject. Here, the first subject is "The manager" (singular, masculine). Therefore, we use the pronoun "he" and the singular auxiliary "has." • The Negative Adverb: The word "rarely" is a semi-negative adverb. If the main statement contains a negative word (seldom, hardly, rarely, barely, never), the question tag must be positive.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. The teacher gave the students a _______ explanation so that everyone could understand the difficult topic easily.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). 📌 Explanation - The sentence provides a context clue: the explanation was given "so that everyone could understand the difficult topic easily." For a difficult topic to become easy for everyone, the explanation must be "Clear." Exam-Oriented Tip: In filler questions, always look for the "Result Clause" (usually starting with so that, resulting in, or making it). Here, the result is "understand easily," which logically pairs with a "clear" explanation. While a "brief" explanation is short, it doesn't necessarily guarantee that a difficult topic will be understood easily. Meanings and Explanation of Options: Clear - स्पष्ट / साफ - Easy to perceive, understand, or interpret. Brief - संक्षिप्त / छोटा - Lasting a short time; using few words. Complex - जटिल - difficult to understand. Harsh - कठोर / रुखा - Unpleasantly rough or jarring to the senses; cruel or severe.

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
options: the salaries of the employees after
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A). 👉 Replace "rise" with "raise" 📌 Error concept: This is an error based on Confusing Verbs. It involves the distinction between an Intransitive Verb (Rise) and a Transitive Verb (Raise). Grammatical concept: • Raise (Transitive Verb): It requires a Direct Object. You raise something (like salaries, hands, or questions). Forms: Raise, Raised, Raised. • Rise (Intransitive Verb): It does not take an object. Something moves upward on its own. Forms: Rise, Rose, Risen

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks. The financial consultant decided to ________ the board members against the merger, but they chose to ignore his professional ________ completely.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). 📌 Explanation - This question tests your ability to distinguish between a Verb and a Noun that sound similar but have different spellings and functions. • In the first blank, we need a Verb following the infinitive "to." The verb form is "Advise" (to give counsel). • In the second blank, we need a Noun following the possessive adjective "professional." The noun form is "Advice" (the counsel given). Advise - (Verb) - To offer suggestions about the best course of action. Example: I advise you to study hard for the SSC CGL exam. Advice - (Noun) - Guidance or recommendations offered with regard to prudent future action. It is an uncountable noun. Example: He gave me some valuable advice on financial planning.

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. P: This shift towards renewable energy is driven by the urgent need to mitigate the catastrophic effects of global warming. Q: Many developed nations have already begun transitioning from fossil fuels to more sustainable alternatives like solar and wind power. R: However, the high initial cost of infrastructure remains a significant hurdle for many developing countries attempting this transition. S: Despite these economic challenges, international climate agreements continue to push for a carbon-neutral future.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A) - QPRS. 📌 Explanation - To determine the logical order, we look for the introduction of the theme and the subsequent flow of arguments: • Finding the Opener: Sentence (Q) is the most suitable starting point as it introduces the general action taking place globally: nations transitioning to sustainable alternatives. • Connecting Q to P: Sentence (P) explains the "Why" behind the shift mentioned in (Q). It identifies the "urgent need to mitigate... global warming" as the primary driver. • Introducing the Contrast (R): Sentence (R) starts with the conjunction "However," introducing a contrasting reality. It points out the "high initial cost" which serves as a hurdle to the transition mentioned in (P). • Concluding with S: Sentence (S) acknowledges the "economic challenges" mentioned in (R) but concludes by stating that international efforts (climate agreements) are still pushing forward. Final Paragraph: Many developed nations have already begun transitioning from fossil fuels to more sustainable alternatives like solar and wind power. This shift towards renewable energy is driven by the urgent need to mitigate the catastrophic effects of global warming. However, the high initial cost of infrastructure remains a significant hurdle for many developing countries attempting this transition. Despite these economic challenges, international climate agreements continue to push for a carbon-neutral future.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. The persistent rains have severely hampered the restoration work of the heritage site.
options: The restoration work of the heritage site is being severely hampered by the persistent rains.
The restoration work of the heritage site has been severely hampered by the persistent rains.
The restoration work of the heritage site was severely hampered by the persistent rains.
The restoration work of the heritage site has severely hampered by the persistent rains.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). 📌 Explanation - The original sentence is in the Active Voice using the Present Perfect Tense (have + V3). When converting this to Passive Voice, the auxiliary verb must change to "has/have + been + V3." 📌 Grammatical concept: • Active Structure: Subject (Persistent rains) + have + V3 (hampered) + Object (restoration work). • Passive Structure: Object (The restoration work) + has been + V3 (hampered) + by + Subject. • Key Rule: The tense must remain constant. "Present Perfect" in Active stays "Present Perfect" in Passive. • Subject-Verb Agreement: In the Passive voice, the verb "has" is used because the new subject "restoration work" is singular, even though the original subject "rains" was plural.

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. The professor said to the students, "Do not forget to submit your research proposals by the end of this week."
options: The professor reminded the students not to forget to submit their research proposals by the end of that week.
The professor told to the students that they should not forget to submit their research proposals by the end of this week.
The professor requested the students that they don't forget to submit their research proposals by the end of that week.
The professor warned the students not to forget to submit your research proposals by the end of that week.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A). 📌 Explanation - This is an Imperative Sentence (a reminder or instruction). When converting from Direct to Indirect speech: • The reporting verb "said to" changes to "reminded" (or told/ordered) based on context. • The negative command "Do not forget" changes to "not to forget." • Pronouns change: "your" becomes "their." • Time expressions change: "this week" becomes "that week."

questions.question: The significance of R. Vaishali winning the Women’s Candidates cannot be overstated. The winner of this ___(1)___ 14-round tournament gets the right to challenge the World champion. It is remarkable that two young Indian women have won two of the biggest events in world chess ___(2)____ a space of nine months. ____(3)____ their successes, and the fact that Indian women are the reigning World team champions, the country cannot boast of great depth in women’s chess. While there is no shortage of talent among boys, the story is different for girls. In the case of the former, generous corporate sponsorship has also played a role. The WestBridge Anand Chess Academy ensured that money was no ___(4)___ for Gukesh’s challenge. To ensure that women’s chess ____(5)___ its momentum, the federation should focus on training girls and organizing more tournaments. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). 💢 Explanation - The context describes a "14-round tournament" that determines who challenges the World champion. Such a long and high-stakes competition is physically and mentally exhausting, making 'Gruelling' - थका देने वाला, अत्यंत कठिन - (extremely tiring and demanding) the perfect fit. • Elementary: Means - प्राथमिक / सरल - basic or simple; a World Championship qualifier is the opposite of basic. • Light: Means - हल्का / आसान - easy or involving little effort; inappropriate for a professional chess tournament. • Pleasant: Means - सुखद / सुहावना - enjoyable or agreeable; while it might be nice, it doesn't describe the intensity of a 14-round battle.

questions.question: The significance of R. Vaishali winning the Women’s Candidates cannot be overstated. The winner of this ___(1)___ 14-round tournament gets the right to challenge the World champion. It is remarkable that two young Indian women have won two of the biggest events in world chess ___(2)____ a space of nine months. ____(3)____ their successes, and the fact that Indian women are the reigning World team champions, the country cannot boast of great depth in women’s chess. While there is no shortage of talent among boys, the story is different for girls. In the case of the former, generous corporate sponsorship has also played a role. The WestBridge Anand Chess Academy ensured that money was no ___(4)___ for Gukesh’s challenge. To ensure that women’s chess ____(5)___ its momentum, the federation should focus on training girls and organizing more tournaments. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A). 💢 Explanation - The sentence refers to the time period during which these two events occurred. 'Within' is used to indicate that something happened inside the limits of a specific period (9 months). • During: Usually refers to a continuous action throughout a period, but "within a space of" is the standard idiomatic expression for time limits. • For: Used for duration (e.g., for nine months), but doesn't fit the structure "a space of." • Between: Requires two points in time (e.g., between January and September).

questions.question: The significance of R. Vaishali winning the Women’s Candidates cannot be overstated. The winner of this ___(1)___ 14-round tournament gets the right to challenge the World champion. It is remarkable that two young Indian women have won two of the biggest events in world chess ___(2)____ a space of nine months. ____(3)____ their successes, and the fact that Indian women are the reigning World team champions, the country cannot boast of great depth in women’s chess. While there is no shortage of talent among boys, the story is different for girls. In the case of the former, generous corporate sponsorship has also played a role. The WestBridge Anand Chess Academy ensured that money was no ___(4)___ for Gukesh’s challenge. To ensure that women’s chess ____(5)___ its momentum, the federation should focus on training girls and organizing more tournaments. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 3 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). 💢 Explanation - The sentence shows a contrast: Indian women are winning big events, BUT the country lacks "depth" in women's chess. 'Despite' - के बावजूद - is a preposition used to introduce a fact that makes the rest of the sentence surprising. • Although: हालांकि / यद्यपि - This is a conjunction and requires a full clause (subject + verb), but here we only have a noun phrase ("their successes"). • Moreover: इसके अलावा - Used to add information, not show contrast. • Nevertheless: फिर भी / तथापि - An adverb used to connect two independent sentences; it cannot be followed directly by a noun phrase here.

questions.question: The significance of R. Vaishali winning the Women’s Candidates cannot be overstated. The winner of this ___(1)___ 14-round tournament gets the right to challenge the World champion. It is remarkable that two young Indian women have won two of the biggest events in world chess ___(2)____ a space of nine months. ____(3)____ their successes, and the fact that Indian women are the reigning World team champions, the country cannot boast of great depth in women’s chess. While there is no shortage of talent among boys, the story is different for girls. In the case of the former, generous corporate sponsorship has also played a role. The WestBridge Anand Chess Academy ensured that money was no ___(4)___ for Gukesh’s challenge. To ensure that women’s chess ____(5)___ its momentum, the federation should focus on training girls and organizing more tournaments. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 4 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). 💢 Explanation - The text mentions that corporate sponsorship ensured that money was not a "limitation" or "restriction" for Gukesh. 'Constraint' means - बाधा, विवशता, प्रतिबंध - limitation or restriction, which fits the financial context perfectly. • Restraint: Usually refers - नियंत्रण / संयम - To self-control or physical prevention of movement. • Constant: Means - निरंतर / स्थिर - Occurring continuously; it doesn't make sense as a noun meaning "obstacle" here. • Compliant: आज्ञाकारी / अनुकूल - This is an adjective meaning "meeting rules/standards"; it doesn't fit the noun requirement.

questions.question: The significance of R. Vaishali winning the Women’s Candidates cannot be overstated. The winner of this ___(1)___ 14-round tournament gets the right to challenge the World champion. It is remarkable that two young Indian women have won two of the biggest events in world chess ___(2)____ a space of nine months. ____(3)____ their successes, and the fact that Indian women are the reigning World team champions, the country cannot boast of great depth in women’s chess. While there is no shortage of talent among boys, the story is different for girls. In the case of the former, generous corporate sponsorship has also played a role. The WestBridge Anand Chess Academy ensured that money was no ___(4)___ for Gukesh’s challenge. To ensure that women’s chess ____(5)___ its momentum, the federation should focus on training girls and organizing more tournaments. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 5 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). 💢 Explanation - The federation wants to ensure that the current momentum (growth/success) continues or is maintained. 'Sustains' means बनाए रखना / जारी रखना - To strengthen or support something to keep it going. • Diminishes: Means - घटाना - to become less; the federation would not want the momentum to decrease. • Subsides: शांत होना / कम हो जाना - Usually used for things like storms or pain becoming less intense; it doesn't fit the context of "momentum." • Refrains: Means परहेज करना - To stop oneself from doing something; it is used for people, not for "momentum."

questions.question: Identify the word that is correctly spelt.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Pharoh Let’s see the meaning of all words: Idiosyncracy - Idiosyncrasy - विचित्र आदत - a peculiar behavior or habit of a person Example: Talking to plants is his idiosyncrasy. Refridgerator - Refrigerator - रेफ्रिजरेटर - an appliance used to keep food and drinks cold Example: We bought a new refrigerator last week. Pharoh - फ़िरऔन - the title of ancient Egyptian kings Example: The Pharaoh was considered a god in ancient Egypt. Disenfranchisment - Disenfranchisement - मताधिकार से वंचित करना - the act of taking away someone's right to vote Example: Disenfranchisement of poor people was common in earlier times.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence – Disillusioned by the boardroom's betrayal, the CFO had to trudge through the litigious aftermath alone.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Lumber Let’s see the meaning of all words: Trudge - थककर भारी कदमों से चलना - to walk slowly with heavy steps, especially because of exhaustion or difficulty Example: After the bus broke down, the passengers had to trudge 8 km to the nearest village in the scorching heat. Lethal - घातक - causing death or able to cause death Example: The bite of a cobra is lethal. Lumber - भारीपन से लड़खड़ाकर चलना - to move in a slow, heavy, awkward way Example: The elephant lumbered towards the water hole. Felon - अपराधी - a person who has committed a serious crime Example: The police arrested the felon after a long chase. Gripe - शिकायत करना - to complain persistently Example: He always gripes about his low salary.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Ensuing
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Former Let’s see the meaning of all words: Ensuing - बाद में होने वाला - happening afterward or as a result Example: The ensuing debate after the speech lasted for hours. Allay - शांत करना - to reduce or calm fear, worry, or difficulty Example: The government tried to allay public fears about inflation. Frugal - मितव्ययी - careful in spending money or using resources Example: My grandfather lived a frugal life and saved a lot. Lucid - स्पष्ट - clearly expressed and easy to understand Example: The professor gave a lucid explanation of quantum physics. Former - पहले का/भूतपूर्व - happening or existing before in time Example: In former times, people used to write letters instead of emails.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. Ivory towers
options: To wait for a short time
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - Detachment and seclusion Let’s see the meaning of idiom: Ivory towers - अलग-थलग और अव्यावहारिक दुनिया - a state of privileged seclusion or separation from the facts and practicalities of the real world Example: Academics are often accused of living in ivory towers, away from real-world problems.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. Phony
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Bogus Let’s see the meaning of all words: Phony - नकली/ढोंगी - not genuine; fake or insincere Example: His excuse for being late sounded phony to me. Teeny - बहुत छोटा - tiny; extremely small Example: She wore a teeny gold ring on her finger. Bogus - झूठा/फर्जी - not genuine or true; fake Example: The company was selling products with bogus claims. Plethora - अधिकता - a large or excessive amount of something Example: The report contained a plethora of data. Lax - लापरवाह - not strict or careful enough Example: Security was very lax at the event.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- Even after losing everything, she maintained a stout belief in justice.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Flimsy Let’s see the meaning of all words: Stout - दृढ़ / मज़बूत - strong and firm in belief or determination Example: Even after losing everything, she maintained a stout belief in justice. Numb - सुन्न - deprived of feeling or responsiveness Example: My fingers went numb in the cold. Zany - मसखरा / अजीब - amusingly unconventional or eccentric Example: The comedian had a zany sense of humor. Flimsy - कमज़ोर / खोखला - lacking strength, weak and unconvincing Example: His excuse for being absent was quite flimsy. Tardy - देर से आने वाला - delaying or delayed beyond the expected time Example: The tardy student missed the first lecture.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. A man who wants to marry a particular woman
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Suitor Let’s see the meaning of all words: Vandal - तोड़फोड़ करने वाला - a person who deliberately destroys or damages property Example: The park was ruined by vandals last night. Dirge - शोकगीत - a mournful song for the dead Example: The choir sang a dirge at the state funeral. Vicar - पादरी - a priest in charge of a parish Example: The vicar conducted the wedding ceremony. Suitor - विवाह का इच्छुक पुरुष - a man who wants to marry a particular woman Example: She rejected all her suitors and chose to remain single.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom. The government forms were full of alphabet soup that common people couldn't understand.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - Incomprehensible or confusing mix Let’s see the meaning of idiom: Alphabet soup - अक्षरों का झमेला - a confusing mixture of abbreviations or initials Example: The finance report was alphabet soup of ROI, EBITDA, EPS, and CAGR that confused the board members.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. Fear of pain
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Algophobia Let’s see the meaning of all words: Algophobia - दर्द का डर - intense, irrational fear of pain Example: Due to algophobia, he avoided going to the dentist for years. Citadel - गढ़ / किला - a fortress protecting a city Example: The ancient citadel stood on the hilltop for centuries. Tutelage - संरक्षण / शिक्षण - protection, instruction, or guidance Example: Under his guru's tutelage, she became a skilled musician. Psephology - चुनाव विज्ञान - the statistical study of elections and voting Example: Psephology helps predict election outcomes using past data.
